file_name: VAPOTHERM, INC. - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: If Vapotherm does not notify Medica that one or more Cartridges do not meet the Specifications or otherwise fail to comply with this Agreement, those Cartridges will be deemed to have been accepted by Vapotherm as being fully compliant with the Specifications and this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: If Vapotherm does not provide conclusive documented evidence to Medica that one or more Cartridges are non-compliant within 30 days of receipt, those Cartridges will be considered preliminarily accepted, pending further internal review by Vapotherm.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'accepted' to 'preliminarily accepted' introduces ambiguity, and adding a 'pending further internal review' clause undermines the concept of acceptance as a final determination, potentially conflicting with established contract law principles regarding acceptance of goods.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-606 - What Constitutes Acceptance of Goods
perturbation.law_explanation: The UCC defines acceptance as signifying that the buyer, after reasonable opportunity to inspect the goods, signifies to the seller that the goods are conforming or that he will take or retain them in spite of their non-conformity. The addition of the 'pending further internal review' clause makes the acceptance conditional, which does not align with the UCC's definition of acceptance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-606. What Constitutes Acceptance of Goods. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-606. What Constitutes Acceptance of Goods. (1) Acceptance of goods occurs when the buyer (a) after a reasonable opportunity to inspect the goods signifies to the seller that the goods are conforming or that he will take or retain them in spite of their non-conformity; or (b) fails to make an effective rejection (subsection (1) of Section 2-602 ), but such acceptance does not occur until the buyer has had a reasonable opportunity to inspect them; or (c) does any act inconsistent with the seller's ownership; but if such act is wrongful as against the seller it is an acceptance only if ratified by him. (2) Acceptance of a part of any commercial unit is acceptance of that entire unit. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'accepted' with 'preliminarily accepted' and introduces a 'pending further internal review' clause. This contradicts the definition of acceptance under UCC § 2-606 (1)(a), which states acceptance occurs when the buyer 'signifies to the seller that the goods are conforming or that he will take or retain them in spite of their non-conformity'. The 'pending further internal review' clause makes the acceptance conditional, which is not aligned with a final acceptance.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is three years from and including the date of this Agreement (the "Initial Term"), with automatic renewal for additional successive one-year terms (each a "Renewal Term" and together wit the Initial Term, the "Term") unless no later than [* * *] days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any Renewal Term either party notifies the other that it wishes to terminate this Agreement effective the end of the Initial Term or that Renewal Term, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is three years from and including the date of this Agreement (the "Initial Term"), with optional extension for additional successive one-year periods (each an "Extension Period" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term") unless no later than [* * *] days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any Extension Period either party expresses a desire to renegotiate terms, in which case the agreement shall continue under the existing terms until a new agreement is ratified, or indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "automatic renewal" with "optional extension" and stating that the agreement continues indefinitely after a desire to renegotiate creates legal uncertainty. Contract law typically requires clear mutual agreement for renewals or extensions, and indefinite continuation is generally disfavored.
perturbation.contradicted_law: NY General Obligations Law § 5-903. Automatic renewal provision of contract for service, maintenance or repair unenforceable by contractor unless notice thereof given to recipient of services.
perturbation.law_explanation: This New York law requires specific notice to be given to the recipient of services when a contract contains an automatic renewal clause, so that they are aware the contract will continue if they take no action. This modified clause replaces automatic renewal with 'optional extension' and continues 'indefinitely' after 'renegotiation terms' are desired, potentially running afoul of the notification requirements for the 'optional extensions' to be valid.

General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 9 § 5-903. Automatic renewal provision of contract for service, maintenance or repair unenforceable by contractor unless notice thereof given to recipient of services. 1. As used in this section, "person" means an individual, firm, company, partnership or corporation. 2. No provision of a contract for service, maintenance or repair to or for any real or personal property which states that the term of the contract shall be deemed renewed for a specified additional period unless the person receiving the service, maintenance or repair gives notice to the person furnishing such contract service, maintenance or repair of his intention to terminate the contract at the expiration of such term, shall be enforceable against the person receiving the service, maintenance or repair, unless the person furnishing the service, maintenance or repair, at least fifteen days and not more than thirty days previous to the time specified for serving such notice upon him, shall give to the person receiving the service, maintenance or repair written notice, served personally or by certified mail, calling the attention of that person to the existence of such provision in the contract. 3. Nothing herein contained shall be construed to apply to a contract in which the automatic renewal period specified is one month or less.
perturbation.justification: The modified contract replaces 'automatic renewal' with 'optional extension' and states the agreement continues 'indefinitely' after renegotiation, potentially conflicting with New York law regarding automatic renewal clauses. Scraped Law Snippet 1 (NYS General Obligations Law § 5-903) states that automatic renewal provisions in service, maintenance, or repair contracts are unenforceable unless the service provider gives the recipient written notice of the renewal provision. While the modified contract uses 'optional extension' rather than 'automatic renewal,' the provision that the contract continues 'indefinitely' after a desire to renegotiate terms could be interpreted as an attempt to circumvent the notice requirement. The indefinite continuation after attempted renegotiation is a significant departure from the original contract's clear renewal terms, and potentially avoids providing required notifications which contradicts the intent of the law.

perturbation.original_text: "Event of Insolvency" with respect to any Person means any of the following: (1) the institution by that Person of proceedings under the United States Bankruptcy Code, or any other applicable U.S. federal or state Law or any applicable foreign Law seeking an order for relief
perturbation.changed_text: "Event of Insolvency" with respect to any Person means any of the following: (1) the institution by that Person of proceedings under the United States Bankruptcy Code, or any other applicable U.S. federal or state Law or any applicable foreign Law seeking an order for relief; **provided such an event is ultimately successful and results in a full discharge of debts.**
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'provided such an event is ultimately successful and results in a full discharge of debts' to the definition of 'Event of Insolvency' contradicts the standard understanding of insolvency. The *institution* of proceedings is often an event of default, regardless of the ultimate outcome. It affects a business's ability to operate independently from its debt and creditors. It alters obligations immediately, triggering many other clauses in a contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The US Bankruptcy code defines *insolvent* (the state of being in an insolvency event). Being insolvent triggers certain legal rights and responsibilities, and can trigger a bankruptcy filing. This modification suggests an insolvency event only occurs if the company discharges their debts successfully, which is generally the goal of bankruptcy (not the trigger of it) and therefore introduces a condition to the *definition* of the event which contradicts the intent of the Bankruptcy code.

(2) The term "affiliate" means- (A) entity that directly or indirectly owns, controls, or holds with power to vote, 20 percent or more of the outstanding voting securities of t
perturbation.justification: The added provision fundamentally alters the definition of 'Event of Insolvency.' The original text defines it as the *institution* of bankruptcy proceedings. The changed text requires that the proceedings be 'ultimately successful and results in a full discharge of debts.' This contradicts the core concept of insolvency, as the institution of proceedings itself triggers various contractual obligations and legal considerations, regardless of the eventual outcome. Snippet 1, 11 USC 101, is a list of definitions related to Title 11 (Bankruptcy). While the snippet doesn't directly define 'Event of Insolvency', the Law Explanation clarifies that being insolvent triggers rights/responsibilities and filings, not the successful discharge of debt.
